Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.backend.ios

Class FlixelIOSLauncher

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.backend.ios.FlixelIOSLauncher
public class FlixelIOSLauncher extends Object
Launches the iOS (RoboVM) version of the FlixelGDX game.

The developer creates a subclass of FlixelGame and an iOS launcher class that extends IOSApplication.Delegate. In IOSApplication.Delegate.createApplication(), create the game instance and return launch(FlixelGame).

  • Method Details

    • launch

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.backends.iosrobovm.IOSApplication launch(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame game)
      Launches the iOS version of the game in RELEASE mode.
      Parameters:
      game - The game instance to launch (e.g. new MyGame(...)).
      Returns:
      The configured IOSApplication to return from createApplication().

    • launch

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.backends.iosrobovm.IOSApplication launch(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame game, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.backend.runtime.FlixelRuntimeMode runtimeMode)
      Launches the iOS version of the game with the given runtime mode.

      Use this from your IOSApplication.Delegate implementation: 

      
 public class MyIOSLauncher extends IOSApplication.Delegate {
   @Override
   protected IOSApplication createApplication() {
     return FlixelIOSLauncher.launch(
         new MyGame("My Game", 800, 600, new InitialState()),
         FlixelRuntimeMode.DEBUG
     );
   }
   public static void main(String[] argv) {
     NSAutoreleasePool pool = new NSAutoreleasePool();
     UIApplication.main(argv, null, MyIOSLauncher.class);
     pool.close();
   }
 }
      Parameters:
      game - The game instance to launch (e.g. new MyGame(...)).
      runtimeMode - The FlixelRuntimeMode for this session (TEST, DEBUG, or RELEASE).
      Returns:
      The configured IOSApplication to return from createApplication().
